function_explanation: Computes the second-order finite difference along the third dimension of a 3D array and applies a shrinkage operation.
function_docstring:
"""
    Computes the second-order finite difference along the third dimension of a 3D array
    and applies a shrinkage operation.

    Args:
        g (np.ndarray): A 3D numpy array of shape (R, N, M) representing the input data.
        byy (np.ndarray): A 3D numpy array of shape (R, N, M) representing the dual variable.
        para (float): A scalar parameter used to scale the result of the backward difference.
        mu (float): A scalar parameter used in the shrinkage operation.

    Returns:
        Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:
            - Lyy (np.ndarray): A 3D numpy array of shape (R, N, M) representing the updated Lagrangian multiplier.
            - byy (np.ndarray): A 3D numpy array of shape (R, N, M) representing the updated dual variable.
    """
usage_example:
import numpy as np

g = np.random.rand(10, 20, 30)
byy = np.zeros((10, 20, 30))
para = 0.5
mu = 1.0

Lyy, updated_byy = iter_yy(g, byy, para, mu)
print(Lyy.shape, updated_byy.shape)
